prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.60) and very high seasonality variance (0.94), with different models excelling at trend tracking vs seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. These patterns strongly support DBA combination which can leverage model diversity while handling disagreement through dynamic weighting.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_CWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "THETA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6001,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.60) and very high seasonality variance (0.94), with different models excelling at trend tracking vs seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. These patterns strongly support DBA combination which can leverage model diversity while handling disagreement through dynamic weighting."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.60) and very high seasonality variance (0.94), with different models excelling at trend tracking vs seasonal capture. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating horizon-specific optimization is valuable. These patterns strongly support DBA combination which can leverage model diversity while handling disagreement through dynamic weighting.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates horizon-specific optimization is valuable. relative_spread_mean=0.27 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=0.60 support DBA combination for handling high disagreement. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination explicitly. With n_windows=3, small top_k=4 provides stability while capturing model diversity. RMSE spread between CWT_catboost (1379.70) and ONLY_FT_rf (2256.03) is 64%, favoring weighted/selection methods over simple mean.
